response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced full-stack and systems engineer, but his background is overwhelmingly web-oriented (TypeScript, Node.js, React) rather than C++-centric. His C++ exposure is limited to approximately six months at National Instruments writing IDE components and a bachelor thesis project using C++ for audio synthesis — neither of which demonstrates the depth in STL, multi-threaded systems, or high-performance networked applications this role demands. There is no evidence of industrial automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL practices, MQTT usage, or any of the industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850) listed as advantageous. The candidate simply does not match the core technical profile.
strengths: Genuine event-driven and distributed systems experience at scale (Dunnhumby micro-services, GISCollective geo-data platform)
Broad multi-language versatility (D, TypeScript, Java, C/C++, Swift) shows adaptability across paradigms
Strong software-craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, mentoring) aligns with quality and documentation expectations
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated: ~6 months at NI plus a bachelor thesis, far short of the 8–12 years of production C++ depth required
No industrial automation or IoT domain knowledge; no mention of IEC 62443, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, MQTT, or any industrial protocol
No indication of Secure Development Lifecycle (SDL) practice or application-security risk management, which is a core responsibility of the role
